title: Meet Your AI Assistant

modules.title: The Day the Chatbot Arrived
modules.sub: A new AI shows up at school — what now?
modules.description: Learners are dropped into the story as a new student encounters the school's AI assistant for the very first time. Through opening choices and observations, they begin forming their first impressions of what an AI assistant looks and feels like in a real setting.

modules.title: What Can It Actually Do?
modules.sub: Testing the AI's real strengths and surprising limits
modules.description: Learners run a series of in-story challenges to discover what the AI assistant genuinely handles well, from drafting and summarising to answering questions. Along the way they start mapping out a mental model of where the tool's power actually begins and ends.

modules.title: When AI Gets It Wrong
modules.sub: Spotting mistakes, hallucinations, and confident nonsense
modules.description: The story takes a turn when the AI produces responses that sound believable but are wrong or made up entirely. Learners identify at least three common failure modes — including hallucination, outdated knowledge, and bias — by catching errors the characters almost missed.

modules.title: The Human Behind the Answer
modules.sub: Understanding who trained the AI and why that matters
modules.description: Learners explore how a large language model is actually built — learning that its responses come from patterns in human-written text, not real understanding or lived experience. This module helps them see why the AI reflects human choices, including human mistakes.

modules.title: Your Turn: Ask It Anything
modules.sub: Practising confident, sceptical use on a real task
modules.description: Learners step out of the story and into a hands-on session where they use a real AI assistant to tackle a task of their own choosing. They apply what they have learned by evaluating the AI's responses critically, deciding what to keep, fix, or throw out entirely.

modules.title: Build Your AI Cheat Sheet
modules.sub: Creating a personal guide for smart AI use
modules.description: Learners synthesise everything from the course by designing their own one-page AI cheat sheet — a practical, personalised reference covering what the tool can do, where it fails, and the questions to ask before trusting any response. The finished sheet is something they can genuinely use going forward.

synopsis: Step inside a story where a new student discovers a school AI assistant and has to figure out when to trust it, when to question it, and when to ignore it entirely. By making real choices alongside the characters, learners build an honest, practical understanding of what ChatGPT and similar tools can and cannot do.
rationale: Middle schoolers are already using AI assistants daily but rarely have structured guidance on how these tools actually function or how to evaluate their outputs critically.
learning_outcome: Students can explain how a large language model generates responses, identify at least three common failure modes, and demonstrate confident, skeptical use of an AI assistant for a real task.
source_signals: Modern-Day Oracles or Bullshit Machines? How to thrive in a ChatGPT world
26% of students ages 13-17 are using ChatGPT to help with homework, study finds
How Students Can Create Notes using BlackBox AI! #shorts #chatgpt #ai #ani #ytshorts
